goal: 支持 Mainline skill 内部分发闭环

summary.title: Mainline skill for internal private-repo distribution
summary.what: Added a skills.sh-compatible Mainline skill at skills/mainline/SKILL.md and narrowed the root binary ignore rule so the skill directory can be tracked.
summary.why: This gives the team an internal minimum loop where the Mainline CLI is installed through Go from the private repository and the Mainline agent workflow can be installed through npx skills before the repository is public.
summary.user_goal: 先按这个方式搞一套出来吧，切个新分支支持一下

decisions.chose: Place the skill inside the existing repository at skills/mainline/SKILL.md.
decisions.rationale: npx skills can discover skills under the default skills/ layout without requiring a separate repository during the internal phase.
rejected: Create a separate skill repository immediately

decisions.chose: Include the private-repo Go install commands in the skill as a prerequisite.
decisions.rationale: The skill teaches agents how to use Mainline but does not install the mainline binary itself.
rejected: Add an npm/npx CLI wrapper in this change

decisions.chose: Document SSH private-repo installation and local-path development validation in the skill.
decisions.rationale: These two paths support the internal closed loop before the repository is public.

decisions.chose: Change the .gitignore bare mainline rule to /mainline.
decisions.rationale: The root build artifact remains ignored while skills/mainline can be tracked. This aligns with prior intent int_76754e76 establishing github.com/mainline-org/mainline as the Go install module path and prior intent int_d46e02c5 establishing the intent-first context commands captured by the skill.

rejected.alternative: Creating a separate skill repository now
rejected.reason: The user asked for the internal loop first, and the current repository can host the single skill under the default skills/ layout.

rejected.alternative: Adding public npm/npx CLI wrapper support now
rejected.reason: The internal phase can use Go's native installer and private GitHub access; public CLI distribution can be added later.

risks: The skill install command that points at the private Git repository requires local GitHub authentication; users without SSH or token access will not be able to install it until the repository is public or another distribution channel exists.
The .gitignore rule change may allow non-root files or directories named mainline to become trackable where they were previously ignored, although the intended root ./mainline binary remains ignored.
followups: After the repository becomes public, verify the short GitHub skills install form and update the public release flow as needed.
Add a public CLI distribution path such as GitHub Releases plus Homebrew or npm wrapper when moving beyond the internal Go-install loop.

architectural_claims: Mainline skill distribution can live in the main repository under the skills/ directory instead of requiring a separate repository.
The skills.sh skill is guidance for agents and remains separate from installation of the mainline CLI binary.
behavioral_changes: npx skills can discover the local Mainline skill source at ./skills/mainline.
The skills/mainline directory is no longer ignored by Git.
security_implications: Private-repo installation depends on the user's existing GitHub SSH or token authorization; no credentials are embedded in the repository.
migration_notes: Existing root mainline build artifacts remain ignored via /mainline.
